commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Needs maintainer sign-off on the auth-provider compatibility tradeoff and optional live AWS default-chain proof rather than a mechanical repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the patch narrows accidental IMDS access and does not add dependencies, scripts, workflows, or secret-handling surfaces. (sha=58572cde36fbaa8a8e0b8e7b5544cedc4522178f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Amazon Bedrock memory credential probe to skip AWS SDK default-chain probing when no AWS credential signals exist, while allowing the full chain for explicitly configured Bedrock memory providers and adding regression tests.

PR surface: Source +134, Tests +128. Total +262 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and dependency inspection rather than a live sandbox run: current main falls through to AWS `defaultProvider()` when static and bearer env credentials are absent, and the locked AWS SDK reaches EC2 IMDS unless disabled. The linked issue adds concrete sandbox logs for the repeated `169.254.169.254` requests.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer sign-off on the auth-provider compatibility tradeoff and optional live AWS default-chain proof rather than a mechanical repair.
